findings.description: The header layout differs: PDF has the seal centered above the department name with Governor/Secretary names flanking, while HTML has the seal left-aligned with the department name centered below and Governor/Secretary names on opposite sides separated by a horizontal rule.
findings.evidence: PDF: seal centered, department name centered below, names flanking. HTML: seal left-aligned, department name large and centered, horizontal rule separator added.

findings.description: Account ID and FEIN fields in the PDF are right-aligned with redacted values; in the HTML they appear right-aligned but without the redacted black bars, just labels with no values.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Account ID: [redacted]' and 'FEIN: [redacted]' with black bars. HTML: 'Account ID:' and 'FEIN:' with no values shown.

findings.description: The redacted (blacked-out) portions throughout the document are not reproduced in the HTML. While these are redactions rather than content, the visual representation differs.
findings.evidence: PDF shows black bars over recipient address, attention line, salutation name, and location names. HTML shows these fields as blank/empty.

findings.description: Footer contact information appears at the bottom of page 1 in the PDF but is rendered at the top of the second HTML page as a header/continuation.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1: footer with phone, fax, address, website at bottom. HTML page 2: same contact info appears at top.

findings.description: The closing signature block in the HTML lacks the redacted name that appears in the PDF above 'Administration Officer'.
findings.evidence: PDF: black redaction bar above 'Administration Officer'. HTML: just 'Administration Officer' with no redaction indicator.

findings.description: The 'cc:' line in the HTML omits the redacted name before 'Director – Sales and Use Tax Division', while the PDF shows a redacted bar.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'cc: [redacted], Director – Sales and Use Tax Division'. HTML: 'cc: , Director – Sales and Use Tax Division'.

findings.description: The document uses h1 for 'North Carolina Department of Revenue' which is reasonable as a document title, but the HTML has nested <main> elements (three <main> tags), which is structurally invalid. There are no other heading levels used in the HTML despite the document having identifiable sub-sections (e.g., 'Re: Private Letter Ruling Request').
findings.evidence: HTML contains three <main> elements nested/sequential. No h2 or lower headings are used for any content sections.

findings.description: The SUPLR 2010-0005 identifier appears in the page header div at the top and again in the page 2 header section, which mirrors the PDF's per-page headers. This is acceptable as it reflects the original document's formatting.
findings.evidence: SUPLR 2010-0005 appears in first page-header div and in the page 2 header div, matching PDF pages.
